titleEn: USPOULTRY processor workshop highlights innovations, excellence and workforce development
titleZh: USPOULTRY家禽加工研讨会聚焦创新、卓越与劳动力发展
summaryZh: 2026年USPOULTRY家禽加工研讨会在南卡罗来纳州格林维尔举行，行业专家探讨了食品安全、加工技术、维护与可靠性系统、劳动力发展及监管举措。Butterball首席运营官强调领导力发展需结合能力、抱负与参与度；Perdue Farms高级总监介绍了结构化维护程序对运营效率的提升；USDA食品安全副部长分享了基于科学的沙门氏菌防控研究。会议为家禽加工企业提供了提升运营效率与食品安全水平的实践策略。
translationZh:
家禽行业专业人士近日齐聚2026年USPOULTRY家禽加工研讨会（南卡罗来纳州格林维尔），共同探讨食品安全、加工技术、维护与可靠性系统、劳动力发展及监管举措的最新进展。通过专家主导的演讲和同行讨论，与会者获得了旨在提升运营效率、强化食品安全计划并支持家禽加工业务长期成功的实用见解与策略。

Butterball LLC首席运营官Neal Walsh强调，领导力发展始于将潜力视为能力、抱负与参与度的结合，并以三脚凳的比喻说明这些特质如何协同支持个人向更广泛职责成长。他还强调了9宫格矩阵作为评估绩效与未来潜力工具的价值。关键要点包括：公平一致评估的重要性、发展责任在个人与组织间的共担，以及将辅导视为持续过程。

在运营效率方面，Perdue Farms维护与可靠性高级总监Gilberto Carrera强调了结构化维护程序在推动运营卓越、最大化产能及通过标准化流程、最佳实践、员工参与和技术优化成本方面的重要性。Carrera指出，有效的维护与可靠性系统计划能够改善资产性能、减少设备停机时间和劳动力需求、支持劳动力发展并创造更大的运营稳定性。

USDA食品安全与检验局副部长Mindy Brashears博士介绍了该机构基于科学的努力——通过数据驱动决策、行业合作和实用食品安全解决方案来减少食源性疾病。Brashears强调了与沙门氏菌检测和定量相关的持续研究与验证工作，以及其聚焦于采样策略、强化危害分析关键控制点的三大支柱方法。
featuredReason: 该研讨会集中展示了家禽加工领域在食品安全、设备维护与劳动力发展方面的最新实践，对智慧畜牧行业人士了解行业趋势、优化运营管理具有参考价值。
contentFull: Associations & FederationsEvents & FeaturesProcessing USPOULTRY processor workshop highlights innovations, excellence and workforce development U.S. Poultry & Egg Association June 17, 2026 0 130 Share FacebookTwitterLinkedinEmailPrintCopy URL Special. Among the speakers at the recent USPOULTRY Poultry Processor Workshop were Neal Walsh, Butterball LLC chief operating officer; and Dr. Mindy Brashears, USDA Food Safety & Inspection Service undersecretary. Must read Three food producers recently issued voluntary recalls and public health alerts July 2, 2026 Resolution reached in DOJ’s egg price manipulation lawsuit July 1, 2026 Eggland’s Best named most trusted egg in America July 1, 2026 Schumpp promoted to Meat Institute VP of international affairs June 30, 2026 Guest Author TUCKER, Ga. — Poultry industry professionals recently gathered at the 2026 USPOULTRY Poultry Processor Workshop in Greenville, S.C., to explore the latest advancements in food safety, processing technologies, maintenance and reliability systems, workforce development and regulatory initiatives. Through expert-led presentations and peer discussions, attendees gained practical insights and strategies designed to enhance operational efficiency, strengthen food safety programs and support long-term business success across poultry processing operations. Neal Walsh, Butterball LLC chief operating officer, emphasized that leadership development begins with recognizing potential as the combination of ability, aspiration and engagement, using the analogy of a three-legged stool to illustrate how those qualities work together to support growth into broader responsibilities. He also highlighted the value of the 9-box matrix as a tool for evaluating both performance and future potential. Key takeaways included the importance of fair and consistent assessments, shared responsibility for development across individuals, and coaching as an ongoing process. Regarding operational efficiencies, Gilberto Carrera, Perdue Farms senior director of maintenance and reliability, emphasized the importance of structured maintenance programs in driving operational excellence, maximizing production capacity and optimizing costs through standardized processes, best practices, employee engagement and technology. Carrera highlighted how effective maintenance and reliability system programs can improve asset performance, reduce equipment downtime and labor requirements, support workforce development and create greater operational stability. Dr. Mindy Brashears, USDA Food Safety & Inspection Service undersecretary, provided an update on the agency’s science-based efforts to reduce foodborne illness through data-driven decision-making, industry collaboration and practical food safety solutions. Brashears highlighted ongoing research and validation work related to Salmonella detection and quantification, as well as its three-pillar approach focused on sampling strategies, strengthening Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) fundamentals and consumer education. Art Lona, Kreative Systems Inc. vice president, discussed ultraviolet (UV) light technology and highlighted its effectiveness as a non-chemical intervention for controlling pathogens in poultry and egg processing. He explained that UV-C light physically destroys microbial DNA, rendering pathogens such as Salmonella, Campylobacter, Listeria monocytogenes, E. coli and others unable to replicate or survive, while leaving no chemical residues on treated products.
